en: Employee consultation on all workplace safety and health matters must be ensured.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 11(4), 13(1)
Defined safety representative duties for advising, supporting, and representing employees on safety and health matters must be established.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 11(1)
Obstacles and depressions must be secured, covered, or marked to prevent hazards.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 2(8)
Adequate ventilation for all rooms based on intended use must be ensured.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 5(3), 26(1)
Hygienic toilets with flushing, toilet paper, and nearby washing facilities must be provided.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 32-34\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 27

Workplace lighting must be sufficient where natural light is lacking. Illumination of at least 30 lux indoors must be provided and glare must be prevented.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 6(5)\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 2(7)(3), 5(1)

Employees must not place themselves in a condition, due to alcohol, medication, or drugs, that could compromise their own safety or the safety of others.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 15(4)\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 5(3)
